freshness_caveat: Coal data as of 2025-12-26 (133 days old); Clean Dark as of 2025-12-31 (128 days old). Coal 8th percentile and Clean Dark 50th percentile figures are stale — do not rely for intraday trading decisions.
risk_flags: Storage at 34.3% (11th percentile, 12.4pp below seasonal) — refill pace critical to Q3 power settlement; current trajectory suggests structural tightness.
Renewable share collapsed to 27% (18th percentile, −33% daily, −41% weekly) — sustained gas-in-the-money regime until wind recovers.
Hormuz escalation and Iran war disrupting regional supply; Trump reopening signal reduces premium, but Libya ramp timeline remains opaque — TTF volatility asymmetric to delays.
EU methane rule weakening may raise upstream cost; indirect upward TTF pressure if supply discipline erodes and leakage increases.

base.summary: Renewables stabilize ~35%; storage injection continues ~2% weekly; Libya ramp signals credible but slow. TTF flat to slightly softer.

downside.summary: Hormuz reopens credibly; SPR drawdown accelerates; Brent falls below $60; LNG arb weakens. Renewables recover above 45%. TTF softens.

upside.summary: Hormuz corridor closure deepens; Iran war escalates regional supply. Libya delay confirmed. TTF tightens; GB power surges.

themes: GB premium and thermal spark in extended territory
Renewables shortage driving thermal merit order
Storage deficit threatens H2 refill trajectory
Geopolitical gas-supply hedging via Libya pivot
top_drivers: Renewable collapse (27%, 18th percentile) forcing gas and coal into baseload; thermal spark at 92nd percentile justified.
Storage deficit (34.3%, 12.4pp below seasonal) — H2 refill risk dominates curve shape; power premium extended.
Iran war and Hormuz disruption vs. Libya gas ramp-up race — geopolitical tail risk on supply diversification timeline.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 93rd percentile, Clean Spark at 92nd; renewables collapsed 33% YoY; storage 12pp below seasonal; Iran war Hormuz risk and Libya gas ramp are the marginal TTF supports.

watchlist.why: Reopening signal lowers Brent risk premium; 17.5M barrel SPR release since March weakens LNG arb into Europe — watch daily Brent-TTF spread.

watchlist.metric: EU storage refill pace & seasonal deficit
watchlist.why: 34.3% fill vs 46.7pp seasonal norm; if Q2 injection slows below 2.5% weekly, summer peak risk escalates and power curve flattens.

watchlist.metric: Libya gas ramp-up and Italy pivot
watchlist.why: Southern European gas hub premium hinges on Tripoli pipeline startup; delays sustain TTF tightness; early milestones due in weeks.
At 18th percentile after 33% daily drop; restoration above 40% would shift coal back into merit and ease thermal spark.

watchlist_dated.name: EU methane rule exemption rollout
watchlist_dated.why: Watch for 'energy security carve-out' invocation; upstream discipline erosion may raise TTF volatility.
